pages.text: Every Skyseed is crafted, then thrown. Each small island stacks four blocks ‚Äî a 2x2 of surface over base; the larger isles take a 3x3. Here is how to make each one, and where to find what it takes. Craft a seed to unlock its field notes.
The $(item)Forest Skyseed$() ‚Äî dirt and planks, both off your $(bold)starter island$(). The first seed you can make.
The $(item)Large Forest Skyseed$() ‚Äî any logs and dirt. Fell a $(item)forest$() first; bigger and pricier.
The $(item)Rocky Skyseed$() ‚Äî stone and cobblestone. Dig into the $(bold)core$() of any grassy island.
The $(item)Large Rocky Skyseed$() ‚Äî stone and cobblestone from any core. A tall mountain that yields $(item)emeralds$().
The $(item)Desert Skyseed$() ‚Äî sand and sandstone. Sand comes off a $(bold)beach or desert$() throw; craft it into sandstone.
The $(item)Large Desert Skyseed$() ‚Äî more sand and sandstone. A vast dune field hiding an oasis.
The $(item)Mushroom Skyseed$() ‚Äî dirt and both mushrooms. They sprout on a $(bold)Forest island's$() shady dark-oak or spruce floor.
The $(item)Large Mushroom Skyseed$() ‚Äî both mushrooms again, grown into a giant grove.
The $(item)Frozen Skyseed$() ‚Äî snow and ice. Gather them off a $(bold)snowy island$() ‚Äî or a Rocky island's snow-capped peak (thrown high or over cold).
The $(item)Large Frozen Skyseed$() ‚Äî snow and packed ice. Snowy or icy by biome, with a frozen lake over frozen ones.
The $(item)Meadow Skyseed$() ‚Äî dirt and any flower. Pick flowers off a $(bold)Forest or flower-forest$() island.
The $(item)Large Meadow Skyseed$() ‚Äî dirt and flowers. A vast bloom ‚Äî the best start for $(item)bees$().
The $(item)Badlands Skyseed$() ‚Äî red sand and terracotta. Red sand comes from a $(bold)Desert seed thrown over a badlands$(); bake clay into terracotta.
The $(item)Large Badlands Skyseed$() ‚Äî red sand and terracotta. A towering, banded mesa rich in gold.
The $(item)Ancient Skyseed$() ‚Äî deepslate and cobbled deepslate. Dig the core of a $(bold)Rocky island thrown deep$(), down near the world's floor.
The $(item)Large Ancient Skyseed$() ‚Äî deepslate from a deep Rocky throw. A huge slab of the deep ‚Äî diamonds and geodes.
The $(item)Lush Skyseed$() ‚Äî moss and azalea. Moss comes from an $(bold)Ancient island$(); the first azalea from a $(bold)Forest's$() bushes.
The $(item)Large Lush Skyseed$() ‚Äî moss and azalea, grown into a bigger isle with a deep, glow-squid pool.
The $(item)Aquatic Skyseed$() ‚Äî clay and sand. Both come from a $(bold)Desert island$(): clay in its core, sand on top.
The $(item)Large Aquatic Skyseed$() ‚Äî clay and sand. A broad island around a proper deep lake.
The $(item)Nether Rocky Skyseed$() ‚Äî netherrack, blackstone and quartz, all dug from a starter $(bold)Nether$() island. The first $(bold)Nether-native$() seed: a full-size mining isle.

The $(item)Nether Lava Skyseed$() ‚Äî blackstone, magma blocks and a $(item)bucket of lava$() (returned empty). A full-size $(bold)lava lagoon$() ‚Äî and the overworld's only lava island, if you throw it topside.
The $(item)Nether Forest Skyseed$() ‚Äî crimson and warped stems, shroomlight and a nether wart block. A full-size $(bold)fungal forest$(): crimson by default, warped in a warped biome.
The $(item)Hamlet Skyseed$() ‚Äî planks, cobblestone and an $(item)emerald$() (mine a Rocky mountain for emeralds). A villager's cottage ‚Äî the start of trading.
The $(item)Trade Post Skyseed$() ‚Äî planks, cobblestone and $(item)3 emeralds$(). A plaza of shops, several trades in one throw.
The $(item)Village Center Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)5 emeralds$(), an iron ingot, planks and cobblestone. All 13 professions and an iron golem ‚Äî the whole economy in one throw.
The $(item)Pasture Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)Wool$() and planks over two dirt. A fenced field of cows, sheep or pigs.
$(italic)Same Pasture Skyseed$(), the other way: $(item)Raw Beef$() in place of the wool, over two dirt. Whichever animal you caught first.
The $(item)Poultry Skyseed$() ‚Äî a $(item)Feather$() and an $(item)Egg$() over two dirt. A walled coop of chickens.
The $(item)Wool Farm Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)two Wool$() over an iron ingot and dirt. A flock in every colour.
The $(item)Stable Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)Leather$() and a $(item)Gold Ingot$() over two planks. Horses, and a chest that might hold a saddle.
The $(item)Aquarium Skyseed$() ‚Äî a $(item)Turtle Scute$() and a $(item)Prismarine Shard$() over two sand. A glass tank of turtles, axolotls and fish.
The $(item)Dungeon Skyseed$() ‚Äî cobblestone around a piece of $(item)rotten flesh$(). A sealed spawner cube with two loot chests.
The $(item)Ruined Portal Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)two obsidian$() over two $(item)gold ingots$(). A broken portal with a chest of Nether spoils.
The $(item)Desert Temple Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)sandstone$() and $(item)TNT$() over two $(item)sand$(). Four chests above a buried cache of TNT.
The $(item)Jungle Temple Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)two mossy cobblestone$() over $(item)jungle planks$() and a $(item)tripwire hook$(). A sealed room with two chests and a dispenser.
The $(item)Witch Hut Skyseed$() ‚Äî a $(item)Cauldron$() and a $(item)brown mushroom$() over two $(item)oak planks$(). A witch, a cat, and brewing drops.
The $(item)Outpost Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)dark oak planks$() around a $(item)crossbow$() and an $(item)iron ingot$(). A pillager watchtower over a caged iron golem, with a loot chest up top.

The $(item)Ocean Monument Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)gold blocks$() and $(item)sponge$() around a $(item)diamond$(). A prismarine temple with guardians, a sponge room and a buried $(item)Heart of the Sea$(). Best thrown over a deep ocean.

The $(item)Trial Chamber Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)tuff bricks$() and $(item)copper blocks$() around a $(item)diamond$(). A buried copper-and-tuff arena of trial spawners and vaults, guarded by a $(item)Breeze$().
The $(item)Woodland Mansion Skyseed$() ‚Äî $(item)dark oak planks$() and $(item)diamonds$() around a $(item)Totem of Undying$(). A two-storey dark-oak manor of vindicators and an evoker.
$(bold)This Almanac$()$(br)Craft any single Skyseed on its own to turn it back into this book ‚Äî so you'll never lose your way.
